title: Workspaces Fitting — tiling resizable panes over referenced view instances

tests.summary: 2 passed — WORKSPACE_LAYOUT_OK (2 instance refs + % geometry persisted via the real store, simulated restart, exact refs+geometry match) + metadata parses / descriptors derive / resolver matches view:any. validate-fitting: PASS all four checks. Full suite 493 passed / 3 skipped.

e2e.summary: 4 passed / 2 skipped (resize drag desktop-scoped by design; render+chrome on all 3 viewports). Sentinels printed: WORKSPACE_PANES_OK, SCREENSHOT docs/autothing/evidence/workspaces-panes.png, CHROME_OK 24 (header 24px <= 28 target). Resize assertion includes reload-persists (re-proves no-save-button in the browser). Zero console errors.

by: lead vision pass on live :7777 + e2e screenshots
designAudit.notes: pane headers thin (24px) in the shell's tan/rule language, hover-revealed close, segmented Garrison|Workspace switch consistent with shell chips. Audit FINDING fixed in-wave: the fitting-overview block above the workspace wasted prime estate — added declarative view-level chrome: full-bleed (UiView.chrome, metadata schema, FittingSurfacePanel) so the workspace owns the viewport; documented in METADATA.md.

video.note: W-wave consolidated walkthrough drives add-pane/resize/reload live

notes: Composes, never owns: panes reference (fittingId, viewId, instanceId); embedded refs mount the SAME registry components, own-port refs iframe the fitting's live url with an honest not-running placeholder; self-nesting refused. Layout is the workspace's own Layer-2 state (workspaces/default.json). Known limits logged: parameterized embedded views (documents:read /:id) show their own no-id empty state when tiled at the default instance; own-port panes require the fitting to be running (pair with W3 eager boot).
